timer_boundary_mapping.excluded_work: decoder iteration, feature extraction, visual_distance, threshold, debounce

timer_boundary_mapping.excluded_work: Pass 1 decoder iteration, Pass 1 feature extraction, deduplication, screenshot output

timer_boundary_mapping.included_work: deduplication logic including extract_features on 84 representative frames
timer_boundary_mapping.excluded_work: Pass 1 decoder iteration and feature extraction, Pass 2 decoder iteration and collection

timer_boundary_mapping.excluded_work: all measured stage timers (roi, extract_features, visual_distance, threshold, debounce, pass2_collect, pass2_dedupe, pass2_screenshots)

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — Packet.decode is called within pyav_iter_frames
profile_overlap_rules.reason: Packet.decode cumulative is a subset of pyav_iter_frames cumulative; adding would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — to_ndarray is called within pyav_iter_frames for sampled frames
profile_overlap_rules.reason: to_ndarray cumulative is a subset of pyav_iter_frames cumulative; would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — compute_histogram is called within extract_features
profile_overlap_rules.reason: compute_histogram cumulative is a subset of extract_features cumulative; would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — cv2_calc_hist is called within compute_histogram for each channel
profile_overlap_rules.reason: cv2_calc_hist cumulative is a subset of compute_histogram cumulative; would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — numpy histogram is the inner implementation of cv2_calc_hist
profile_overlap_rules.reason: numpy histogram cumulative is a subset of cv2_calc_hist cumulative; would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: nested_cumulative — cv2_to_gray is called within extract_features
profile_overlap_rules.reason: cv2_to_gray cumulative is a subset of extract_features cumulative; would double-count

profile_overlap_rules.relationship: serial_synchronous — all execute sequentially in the same thread within the for timestamp, image in frames loop
profile_overlap_rules.reason: These are all serial stages; Packet.decode and extract_features do NOT execute concurrently. The for loop advances only when the body completes.

pipeline_execution_model.description: pyav_iter_frames is a Python generator. detect_changes consumes it in a synchronous for loop. Each iteration: (1) the generator yields the next sampled VideoFrame via builtins.next, (2) ROI + extract_features + visual_distance + threshold execute in the same thread, (3) the loop body completes before the next generator advance. All processing is single-threaded: no worker pool, no queue, no concurrent decode/extract.

candidate_evaluation.profile_evidence: 92.712s cumulative, 0.039s self, 1285 calls. Subsystem: compute_histogram 48.842s cumulative (40.567s numpy histogram), cv2_to_gray 34.049s cumulative.
candidate_evaluation.source_boundary: slide_detector.detect_changes, measure('extract_features'), synchronous call to frame_features.extract_features on ROI-cropped image
candidate_evaluation.strongest_supporting: Largest single measured timer. Profile confirms compute_histogram + cv2_to_gray are the dominant sub-components. Called on every sampled frame (1201 Pass 1 + 84 dedupe).
candidate_evaluation.strongest_counter: Residual (87.39s, 32.96%) is nearly as large and likely contains first-pass decode. Without isolating decode wall-clock, cannot confirm extract_features is larger than decode.
candidate_evaluation.ruling: POSSIBLE_PRIMARY — largest measured timer but cannot be safely confirmed due to uninstrumented decode in residual

candidate_evaluation.wall_clock_evidence: No independent non-overlapping timer exists. First-pass decode lives in unattributed_residual (87.39s, 32.96%).
candidate_evaluation.profile_evidence: Packet.decode 114.854s self/cumulative (profile run). pyav_iter_frames 135.128s cumulative, 10.469s self.
candidate_evaluation.source_boundary: pyav_backend.pyav_iter_frames — Packet.decode loop + sampling + to_ndarray for selected frames. Called from video_decode.iter_frames wrapped in InstrumentedIterator.
candidate_evaluation.strongest_supporting: Profile shows Packet.decode (114.854s) larger than extract_features (92.712s cumulative). Residual (87.39s) is large and likely contains first-pass decode work (aggregate note: decode/open/service/persistence and other uninstrumented work).
candidate_evaluation.strongest_counter: The profile run is a separate instrumented run (229.750s) with a different elapsed time from the median recorded run (265.125s); cProfile values are supporting evidence and cannot substitute for independently isolated median wall-clock stage timers. No non-overlapping wall-clock timer isolates decode. Pass2_collect (65.22s, 24.6%) includes second-pass decode + collection and is still smaller than extract_features.
candidate_evaluation.ruling: POSSIBLE_PRIMARY — profile suggests decode is comparable to feature extraction, but no wall-clock timer independently validates this

candidate_evaluation.profile_evidence: Pass 2 iterates decoder again (1201 frames sampled, 2402 ndarray conversions = Pass 1 + Pass 2). Second decoder iteration is included in this timer.
candidate_evaluation.source_boundary: Mixed timer: second decoder iteration (Packet.decode + to_ndarray) + segment loop + representative matching + ROI processing + rep_frames insertion
candidate_evaluation.strongest_supporting: Second-largest measured stage at 24.6% of total.
candidate_evaluation.strongest_counter: This is a MIXED timer. The pure collection overhead (segment scanning, matching, dict insertion) is NOT independently measured — it is entangled with second-pass decode/frame advancement (Packet.decode, to_ndarray) within the same timer. The internal proportion between collection cost and decode cost is UNKNOWN. Cannot be selected as primary because the timer does not isolate collection from decode.
candidate_evaluation.ruling: NOT_PRIMARY — mixed timer; decode portion is entangled with collection overhead; cannot isolate pure collection cost

candidate_evaluation.profile_evidence: Not visible in top profile entries — negligible.

candidate_evaluation.wall_clock_evidence: Residual 87.38648949978233s (32.96%). Combined extract_features (101.69s) + residual (87.39s) = 189.08s (71.3%) vs pass2_collect (65.22s, 24.6%).
candidate_evaluation.profile_evidence: Packet.decode (114.854s) and pyav_iter_frames (135.128s cumulative) live outside measured timers.
candidate_evaluation.source_boundary: residual = detect_elapsed - measured_stage_total. Contains uninstrumented first-pass decode, decoder/container setup, metadata, segment building, service/repository overhead, document construction, persistence.
candidate_evaluation.strongest_supporting: Honest assessment: the primary-wallclock cost could be split between extract_features and first-pass decode, with neither independently confirmed larger.
candidate_evaluation.strongest_counter: N/A — this is the fallback when specific discrimination fails.
candidate_evaluation.ruling: BLOCKED — evidence cannot safely discriminate between FEATURE_EXTRACTION_CPU and DECODE_FRAME_PIPELINE without additional wall-clock instrumentation

confidence_rationale: Extract_features (101.69s, 38.4%) is the largest instrumented non-overlapping timer. However, the unattributed residual (87.39s, 33.0%) likely contains first-pass decode work, and the cProfile profile run (229.8s) shows Packet.decode (114.854s) exceeding extract_features cumulative (92.712s). Without separate non-overlapping wall-clock timers for pass1_decode, pass2_decode, and pass2_match_and_collect, we cannot safely determine whether FEATURE_EXTRACTION_CPU or DECODE_FRAME_PIPELINE is the primary bottleneck.

step18_5_acceptance.decoder_call_semantics: preserved unless optimization explicitly targets decode architecture with new parity proof
step18_5_acceptance.benchmark_protocol: one warmup, three recorded runs, one separate profile
step18_5_acceptance.optimization_parity_gate: exact canonical signature match required — quality regression is not permitted as a shortcut

evidence_gap_description: Missing independent non-overlapping wall-clock instrumentation for: pass1_decode_or_frame_advance (first-pass Packet.decode + pyav_iter_frames overhead), pass2_decode_or_frame_advance (second-pass decode separate from segment matching and collection). Without these timers, residual (87.4s, 33.0%) cannot be attributed and safe discrimination between FEATURE_EXTRACTION_CPU and DECODE_FRAME_PIPELINE is impossible.
